CheckTitle: Default app management policy enforces credential restrictions on applications and service principals
Description: This check verifies that the **default app management policy** in Microsoft Entra ID has all required **credential restrictions** configured for applications: **block password addition**, **restrict max password lifetime**, **block custom passwords**, **block custom identifier URIs**, **block identifier URIs without unique tenant identifiers**, and **restrict max certificate lifetime**.
Risk: Without the required credential restrictions, applications and service principals can use **insecure credential configurations**, including **long-lived secrets**, **custom passwords**, **non-unique identifier URIs**, or **unrestricted certificates**, increasing the risk of **credential compromise** and **unauthorized access**.

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the **Microsoft Entra admin center** (https://entra.microsoft.com/).
2. Go to **Identity** > **Applications** > **App management policies**.
3. Select the **Default app management policy**.
4. Under **Password restrictions**, enable: **Block password addition**, **Restrict max password lifetime**, and **Block custom passwords**.
5. Under **Identifier URI restrictions**, enable: **Block custom identifier URIs** and **Block identifier URIs without unique tenant identifiers**.
6. Under **Certificate restrictions**, enable: **Restrict max certificate lifetime**.
7. Save the changes.

Recommendation.Text: Configure the **default app management policy** with all 6 credential restrictions: **block password addition**, **restrict max password lifetime**, **block custom passwords**, **block custom identifier URIs**, **block identifier URIs without unique tenant identifiers**, and **restrict max certificate lifetime**.

Notes: The default app management policy helps enforce secure credential practices across all applications in the tenant. Each restriction type can be individually configured and enabled. The identifier URI restrictions correspond to the `symmetricKeyAddition` and `symmetricKeyLifetime` restriction types in the Microsoft Graph API.
